data.title: Fastly gets a rating cut at Craig-Hallum
data.description:
Craig-Hallum downgraded Fastly (FSLY) to Hold from Buy but increased the price target on the stock to $24 from $15, noting that it awaits better risk-reward as AI impact looms.

Shares of Fastly climbed about 2% premarket on Tuesday. 

Analysts led by Jeff Van Rhee said risk/reward now appears considerably less attractive as a range of risks are rising in relevance.

 "For the negatives/concerns: first, capex (and increasingly, margin) is going to be challenged by surging prices for memory and other key network hardware, as demand from Al infrastructure buildouts exceeds supply. Second, Al has now clearly surpassed human capabilities with respect to detection and exploitation of security vulnerabilities in virtually all systems, making it increasingly hard to charge for value-add services layered on top of the core network (i.e. WAF, etc), with that value more likely now to accrue to leading frontier models (security currently 22% of revenue)," said the analysts.

Thirdly, the analysts noted that compares will get more difficult, mainly as Edgio tailwinds abate. In December 2024, Akamai Technologies acquired select assets of Edgio, leading many customers to churn off, likely landing on Fastly's platform at renewal time, the analysts added.

In addition, the analysts said that the magnitude of improvement in AI represented by Anthropic's Mythos (over the already surprising November 25 release of Opus 4.5) not only casts doubt on the leading security solutions, but has also set a new slope to the curve of improvement in AI capabilities, meaningfully increasing the probability of disruption over the next few releases as they again make unexpected leaps.

The analysts also highlighted some positives. 

Firstly, Van Rhee and his team said that pricing remains compelling, with mid-single-digit declines yielding the best gross margin in years. Secondly, the analysts noted that remaining performance obligations, or RPOs, have surged, with at least a meaningful portion related to efforts to extract higher minimum-commitments from customers (increasing visibility). Thirdly, the analysts noted that Al is comprising an increased percentage of overall traffic, as bots/agents perform tasks

"In sum, the company has done a very nice job executing, integrating product and improving go-to-market. At the same time, we are stepping aside as much appears reflected in the current valuation and we await a more attractive risk/reward entry," said Van Rhee and his team.

data.title: Camtek to acquire Visual Layer to expand AI inspection capabilities
data.description:
• Camtek (CAMT) on Tuesday said it has signed a definitive agreement to acquire Visual Layer, a Tel Aviv-based artificial intelligence firm specializing in visual analytics.
  • Camtek said the acquisition will strengthen its inspection and metrology offerings for the semiconductor industry by integrating Visual Layer’s AI and visual intelligence technologies.
  • The company said it has already collaborated with Visual Layer for over a year and expects the deal to close in the coming weeks, subject to customary conditions.
  • Financial terms were not disclosed.
  • CAMT +5.52% premarket to $184.6.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CareCloud secures $50M credit facility, redeems preferred stock
data.description:
• CareCloud (CCLDO) on Tuesday said it has secured a $50M credit facility with Citizens Bank and Provident Bank, while also announcing plans to fully redeem its Series B preferred stock.
  • The company said the credit facility, led by Citizens Bank, will support its operations and growth initiatives.
  • CareCloud also said it will redeem all outstanding Series B preferred shares on May 15, eliminating about $3.2 million in annual dividend obligations and simplifying its capital structure.
  • The company said the moves are aimed at lowering financing costs, improving liquidity, and supporting expansion of its AI-driven healthcare technology platform.
  • CCLD -1.42% premarket to $2.76.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BlackRock's iShares just had its best Q1 ever: Here are the 10 top-rated funds in the lineup
data.description:
BlackRock's iShares unit just posted a record-breaking Q1, pulling in $132 billion in net inflows, the highest Q1 figure in iShares history, as part of a strong overall earnings beat for parent company BlackRock (BLK), which reported 8% year-over-year organic fee growth and adjusted margins expanding by more than 100 basis points. 

In light of this, below is a list of the top 10 iShares ETFs ranked according to their Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings. These traditional BlackRock fund products span various sectors including Information Technology, Natural Resources, Gold & Precious Metals, and regional equity markets such as South Korea, Brazil, and Austria.

The list is led by the iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX), which earned a near-perfect Quant Rating of 4.88. The iShares MSCI South Korea ETF (EWY) follows closely behind with a score of 4.85, while the iShares MSCI Peru and Global Exposure ETF (EPU) rounds out the top three with a rating of 4.69.

Metals and mining ETFs are well represented on the list, with the iShares MSCI Global Silver and Metals Miners ETF (SLVP) and the iShares Copper and Metals Mining ETF (ICOP) both earning Strong Buy ratings. Regional ETFs focused on Poland (EPOL), Brazil (EWZ), and Austria (EWO) also made the top ten, each earning solid Buy ratings above 4.4.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system grades ETFs based on their relative performance on various critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, momentum, and profitability. Ratings are given on a scale of 1 to 5, with any score of 3.5 or above considered bullish and any rating of 2.5 or below considered bearish.

Here is the list:

  1. iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX), Quant Rating: 4.88

  2. iShares MSCI South Korea ETF (EWY), Quant Rating: 4.85

  3. iShares MSCI Peru and Global Exposure ETF (EPU), Quant Rating: 4.69

  4. iShares MSCI Global Silver and Metals Miners ETF (SLVP), Quant Rating: 4.61

  5. iShares US Oil Equipment & Services ETF (IEZ), Quant Rating: 4.57

  6. iShares MSCI Global Gold Miners ETF (RING), Quant Rating: 4.52

  7. iShares Copper and Metals Mining ETF (ICOP), Quant Rating: 4.51

  8. iShares MSCI Poland ETF (EPOL), Quant Rating: 4.46

  9. iShares MSCI Brazil ETF (EWZ), Quant Rating: 4.45

  10. iShares MSCI Austria ETF (EWO), Quant Rating: 4.44

data.title: Lilly required by FDA to run post-marketing studies on obesity pill
data.description:
• The U.S. Food and Drug Administration ((FDA)) has set a post-marketing requirement for Eli Lilly (LLY) regarding its newly approved weight loss pill, orforglipron, branded as Foundayo.
  • According to a Reuters report on Tuesday, the agency requires the Indiana-based LLY to conduct post-market trials for Foundayo to evaluate the GLP-1 product for risks related to liver ​injury, ​cardiovascular events, gastric ‌emptying, ⁠and milk-only lactation.
  • The requirement comes days after the FDA approved the once-daily pill for use alongside a lower-calorie diet and increased physical activity for weight management in overweight and obese adults with weight-related medical issues.
  • Last week, Eli Lilly (LLY) launched Foundayo across U.S. retail pharmacies in a market dominated by Novo Nordisk (NVO), which received FDA approval for its oral weight loss therapy, the Wegovy pill, in December.

data.title: Bloom Energy poised to open at all-time high after expanded Oracle pact
data.description:
Bloom Energy (BE) up 14.5% pre-market Tuesday, indicated to open at an all-time high near $200, after securing an expanded partnership with Oracle (ORCL) in which the tech giant can procure up to 2.8 GW of Bloom's fuel cell capacity, with an initial 1.2 GW already contracted and deployment actively underway.

The two companies first came together in July, when Bloom (BE) said it would deliver energy to Oracle (ORCL) data centers in the U.S. within 90 days.

Oracle (ORCL) also holds a warrant to purchase up to 3.53M Bloom (BE) shares at $113.28/share, for a total investment of $400M; assuming Oracle exercises the warrant, its investment is already up by more than $300M.

Bloom's (BE) expanded partnership is an "additional stamp of approval for continued momentum," and the scale of the agreement, "in addition to BE's already strong backlog, could justify further capacity expansion, a potential catalyst," according to Baird analyst Ben Kallo, who rates the stock at Outperform.

"The expanded agreement with Oracle is a clear signal that hyperscale AI demand is translating directly into gigawatt-scale," Evercore ISI analyst Nicholas Amicucci wrote; he rates Bloom (BE) at Outperform.

Nuclear-related stocks are rising pre-market, as the technology becomes a more attractive fuel source for major AI players: Oklo (OKLO) up 8.9%, NuScale Power (SMR) up 6.6%, Nano Nuclear Energy (NNE) up 5.8%, Lightbridge (LTBR) up 4.6%, Centrus Energy (LEU) up 3.8%, Energy Fuels (UUUU) up 2.9%, NexGen Energy (NXE) up 1.8%.

data.title: BT Brands says merger partner Aero Velocity ties up with AC Future on mobile drone platform
data.description:
• BT Brands (BTBD) on Tuesday said its proposed merger partner Aero Velocity has entered a strategic partnership with AC Future, to develop a mobile drone launch vehicle platform for U.S. military and public-sector use.
  • The company said the platform is intended to provide a mobile, self-contained system for drone launch, recovery and command operations in infrastructure-limited environments.
  • Planned capabilities include multi-drone launch and storage, onboard power systems, secure communications and modular payload configurations to support missions such as surveillance, logistics and emergency response.
  • BT Brands said the partners plan to pursue U.S. government funding opportunities and explore commercial and public safety applications.
  • BTBD +14.61% premarket to $1.96.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Goldman Sachs declares $4.50 dividend
data.description:
• Goldman Sachs (GS) declares $4.50/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.02%
  • Payable June 29; for shareholders of record June 1; ex-div June 1.
  • See GS Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Pantheon Macro flags lackluster Q1 GDP as underlying demand weakens
data.description:
U.S. economic growth appears to have remained subdued in the first quarter, with early estimates pointing to a modest expansion following an already weak finish to last year, according to analysis from Pantheon Macroeconomics.

The investment firm noted that gross domestic product is tracking at roughly a 1.8% annualized pace, signaling only a slight improvement from the 0.5% growth recorded in the fourth quarter. The underlying composition of growth suggests limited momentum, Pantheon added.

Consumer spending showed signs of softness, while net trade weighed heavily on overall output, offsetting some of the gains seen elsewhere. Government spending provided a partial cushion, rebounding after prior weakness and helping to stabilize headline figures. 

Pantheon notes that while some sluggishness in late 2024 may be attributed to residual seasonality, this factor explains only a portion of the broader slowdown. Incoming data for January and February, along with early indicators for March, have provided sufficient visibility to form a preliminary outlook ahead of the official GDP release scheduled for April 30. 

Still, upcoming reports on durable goods, inventories, and trade could meaningfully influence the final estimate, leaving room for revisions, the financial institution indicated. 

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: Corteva unveils leadership team for planned seed and genetics spin-off
data.description:
Corteva Inc. (CTVA) on Tuesday named the executive leadership team that will run its planned seed and genetics spin-off, currently referred to as “SpinCo,” ahead of a targeted separation in the fourth quarter of 2026.

Chief Executive Chuck Magro is set to lead the new publicly traded company as chief executive following the split.

The announcement outlines a senior team that includes David Johnson as chief financial officer, Judd O’Connor as chief commercial and operations officer, and Sam Eathington as chief technology officer, along with Audrey Grimm as chief people officer, Brian Lutz as chief digital and information officer and Jennifer Johnson as chief legal officer.

The spin-off is expected to focus on seed and crop genetics, including areas such as gene editing and molecular breeding, while maintaining Corteva’s (CTVA) existing germplasm base.

The company plans to expand commercialization and licensing efforts and explore applications beyond traditional row crops.

The leadership team will formally assume their roles upon completion of the separation, and executives are scheduled to host an investor day at the New York Stock Exchange on September 15, with additional details, including the new company’s name and branding, to be announced.
